# Jatin Thummar — Frontend Engineer & Mobile App Developer

> Jatin Thummar is a Frontend Engineer based in India, specializing in building scalable cross-platform mobile applications for Android, iOS, and Web using React Native, Expo, TypeScript, and Next.js. He has production experience in e-commerce, Shopify app development, and mobile-first product engineering.

## About

Jatin Thummar builds production-grade cross-platform apps with React Native, Expo, TypeScript, and Next.js. He focuses on clean code, thoughtful frontend architecture, and smooth user experiences.

- Role: Frontend Engineer & Mobile App Developer
- Company: The Korean Fashion — building and scaling a cross-platform e-commerce mobile app, shipping new features, improving performance, and refining the developer experience
- Location: India
- Specializations: Cross-platform mobile development, React Native, Expo, Shopify mobile apps, e-commerce, frontend architecture, TypeScript
- Tech stack: React Native, Expo, TypeScript, JavaScript, Next.js, React, Tailwind CSS, Astro, Node.js, Git, Vite, GitHub Actions
- Website: https://jatinthummar.github.io
- GitHub: https://github.com/JatinThummar
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/jatinthummar
- X (Twitter): https://x.com/jatinthummarx

### How I Work

I care about the details — clean code, thoughtful architecture, and smooth user experiences. I believe great apps come from understanding both the technical and the human side of development. I prefer keeping things simple and intentional. Whether it's choosing the right abstraction, optimizing a critical render path, or picking the right tool for the job — I lean towards solutions that are practical, not over-engineered.

### Projects

**FlipBitz — Feed App** (Personal Project): Cross-platform feed app for iOS, Android & Web built with React Native and Expo. Real-time news feeds, dynamic color themes, and light/dark mode. https://flipbitz.netlify.app/web/

**LEO Mobile App Builder** (Lead Developer · Softpulse Infotech · 2023–2024): No-code mobile app builder for Shopify stores, built with React Native and Expo. Merchants create and publish their own e-commerce app to iOS and Android — no coding required. https://leoapps.io/

**As a Man Thinketh** (Personal Project): Static reading experience for James Allen's classic book, built with Astro. Translated into multiple languages including English, Gujarati, and Hindi. https://jatinthummar.github.io/thinketh/

---

## Blog: UI/UX Design Resources for Developers

A curated collection of tools, courses, inspiration sites, books, and communities to help you learn UI/UX design.

You don't need a design degree to build beautiful, usable interfaces. These are the resources I've found most useful for learning UI/UX — from theory and inspiration to hands-on tools and practice challenges.

### Courses & Learning Platforms

- Google UX Design Course (https://grow.google/intl/en_in/ux-design-course/) — Google's professional certificate on Coursera. Covers the full UX process from research to prototyping.
- Interaction Design Foundation (https://www.interaction-design.org/) — One of the most comprehensive UX education platforms.
- Hack Design (https://hackdesign.org/lessons) — Free design lessons curated by top designers, delivered weekly.
- IBM Design Thinking (https://www.ibm.com/design/thinking/page/courses/Practitioner) — IBM's free practitioner course on applying design thinking at scale.
- Baseline HQ (https://www.baselinehq.com/course.html) — Typography-focused design course.
- UXPin Ebooks (https://www.uxpin.com/studio/ebooks/) — Free ebooks on UX design, prototyping, and design systems.
- Egghead (https://egghead.io/) — Expert-led courses for front-end web developers.
- Design Better (https://www.designbetter.co/) — Discover the world's best design practices.

### UX Theory & Principles

- Laws of UX (https://lawsofux.com/) — The essential psychological principles that every designer should know.
- Principles.design (https://principles.design/) — Open source collection of design principles.
- NN Group (https://www.nngroup.com/articles/) — The gold standard for evidence-based UX research and guidelines.
- Psychology of Design — 106 Cognitive Biases (https://growth.design/psychology#hicks-law) — Visual reference of cognitive biases that affect design decisions.

### Design Systems

- Material Design 3 (https://m3.material.io/) — Google's design system.
- Apple Human Interface Guidelines (https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/) — Apple's design bible.
- Shopify Polaris (https://polaris.shopify.com/) — One of the best-documented design systems.
- Carbon Design System (https://carbondesignsystem.com/) — IBM's open-source design system.
- Design Systems Repo (https://designsystemsrepo.com/design-systems/) — Searchable directory of open-source design systems.

### Case Studies

- Growth.Design (https://growth.design/case-studies) — UX case studies told as visual comic strips.
- Baymard — E-Commerce Checkout Usability (https://baymard.com/research/checkout-usability) — Original research on checkout UX.

### Inspiration & UI Galleries

- Dribbble (https://dribbble.com/) — The go-to for visual design inspiration.
- Behance (https://www.behance.net/) — Full case studies and project showcases.
- Mobbin (https://mobbin.com/browse/ios/apps) — Real-world mobile app screenshots.
- Land-book (https://land-book.com/) — Hand-picked landing page designs.
- Refero Design (https://refero.design/) — Real product screenshots from top companies.

### Design & Prototyping Tools

- Figma (https://www.figma.com) — The industry standard for UI design.
- Framer (https://www.framer.com/) — Design and ship real websites with no code.
- Excalidraw (https://excalidraw.com/) — Virtual whiteboard for hand-drawn diagrams.

### Color & Typography Tools

- Coolors (https://coolors.co/) — Fast color palette generator.
- FontJoy (https://fontjoy.com/) — AI-powered font pairing generator.
- Google Fonts (https://fonts.google.com/) — The free font library.
- UI Colors (https://uicolors.app/create) — Tailwind CSS color palette generator.

### Books

- Hooked — Nir Eyal: The framework for building habit-forming products.
- Articulating Design Decisions: How to communicate and defend your design choices.
- Steal Like an Artist — Austin Kleon: A creative manifesto for finding inspiration everywhere.

---

## Blog: AI Tools & Learning Resources

Courses, blogs, newsletters, and people to follow for learning AI engineering, prompt design, and building with LLMs.

AI is reshaping how we build software. Whether you're integrating LLMs into your app, writing better prompts, or just trying to keep up — these are the resources I've found most useful.

### Courses & Documentation

- Anthropic Courses (https://github.com/anthropics/courses) — Free interactive courses on prompt engineering, Claude API, and building with MCP.
- Anthropic Learn (https://www.anthropic.com/learn) — Official learning resources and guides from Anthropic.
- DeepLearning.AI Short Courses (https://www.deeplearning.ai/short-courses/) — 88+ short courses on GenAI, LangChain, RAG, and more.
- OpenAI Cookbook (https://github.com/openai/openai-cookbook) — Examples and guides for using the OpenAI API.
- Epic AI (https://www.epicai.pro/) — Kent C. Dodds' course on building AI-powered apps with MCP.
- Google AI for Developers (https://ai.google.dev/) — Gemini API docs, tutorials, and quickstarts.
- Microsoft Generative AI for Beginners (https://github.com/microsoft/generative-ai-for-beginners) — 21 lessons to get started building with generative AI.
- Prompt Engineering Guide (https://www.promptingguide.ai/) — Comprehensive open-source guide to prompt engineering.

### AI Coding Tools & Assistants

- Claude Code (https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) — Anthropic's CLI coding assistant.
- Cursor (https://www.cursor.com/) — AI-first code editor built on VSCode.
- GitHub Copilot (https://github.com/features/copilot) — AI pair programmer from GitHub.

### People to Follow

- Andrej Karpathy (https://karpathy.ai/) — Founding member of OpenAI, former Director of AI at Tesla.
- Simon Willison (https://simonwillison.net/) — Co-creator of Django, now focused on LLMs.
- Andrew Ng (https://www.andrewng.org/) — Founder of DeepLearning.AI and Coursera.
- Lilian Weng (https://lilianweng.github.io/) — Head of Safety Systems at OpenAI.
- Chip Huyen (https://huyenchip.com/) — Author of Designing Machine Learning Systems.

### Engineering Blogs — AI Teams

- OpenAI Blog (https://openai.com/blog) — Research updates, model releases, and technical deep dives.
- Anthropic Research (https://www.anthropic.com/research) — Claude model updates and interpretability research.
- Google DeepMind Blog (https://deepmind.google/discover/blog/) — Gemini, AlphaFold, and frontier AI research.
- Hugging Face Blog (https://huggingface.co/blog) — Open-source models and transformers library updates.

### Newsletters

- The Batch (https://www.deeplearning.ai/the-batch/) — Andrew Ng's weekly newsletter.
- TLDR AI (https://tldr.tech/ai) — Daily AI, ML, and data science updates in 5 minutes.
- Import AI (https://importai.substack.com/) — Weekly deep dives into AI research and policy.
- Ahead of AI (https://magazine.sebastianraschka.com/) — Sebastian Raschka's newsletter on LLMs and deep learning.

### Open Source & Tools

- Hugging Face (https://huggingface.co/) — The GitHub of AI. Models, datasets, and spaces.
- LangChain (https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain) — Framework for building LLM-powered applications.
- Ollama (https://ollama.com/) — Run open-source LLMs locally.
- Instructor (https://github.com/jxnl/instructor) — Structured outputs from LLMs using Pydantic.

### Books

- Designing Machine Learning Systems — Chip Huyen: The practical guide to building ML systems in production.
- Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch) — Sebastian Raschka: Understand LLMs by building one step by step.
- AI Engineering — Chip Huyen: Building applications with foundation models.

---

## Blog: Thinkers & Writers Worth Reading

Founders, thinkers, and writers who changed how I see tech, startups, and life.

Not everything worth reading is about code. These are the founders, investors, and thinkers whose writing has shaped how I think about building products, startups, and life in general.

### Startups & Venture

- Naval Ravikant (https://nav.al/) — Wealth, leverage, and clear thinking.
- Paul Graham (http://www.paulgraham.com/) — Co-founder of Y Combinator. Essays on startups and unconventional thinking.
- Sahil Lavingia (https://sahillavingia.com/) — Founder of Gumroad. Honest writing about bootstrapping.
- Ryan Hoover (https://www.ryanhoover.me/) — Founder of Product Hunt.

### Building & Business Philosophy

- Jason Fried (https://world.hey.com/jason) — Co-founder of Basecamp and Hey. Calm companies and remote work.
- DHH (https://dhh.dk/) — Co-founder of Basecamp, creator of Ruby on Rails.
- Patrick Collison (https://patrickcollison.com/) — Co-founder and CEO of Stripe.
- Nathan Barry (https://nathanbarry.com/) — Founder of ConvertKit.

### Writing & Thinking

- David Perell (https://perell.com/) — Essays on writing online and the knowledge economy.
- Farnam Street (https://fs.blog/blog/) — Mental models, decision-making, and clear thinking.
- Julian Shapiro (https://www.julian.com/) — Handbooks on writing, startup growth, and more.

---

## Blog: Frontend Tools & Learning Resources

Tools, communities, and platforms that helped me grow as a frontend developer.

### React

- Awesome React (https://awesomereact.com/) — The best React videos and playlists on the web.
- React Resources (https://reactresources.com/) — Curated React articles, tutorials, tools, and libraries.
- Reactiflux (https://www.reactiflux.com/) — One of the largest React communities.
- Awesome React Components (https://github.com/brillout/awesome-react-components) — Curated list of React components.

### Next.js

- Next Radar (https://nextradar.dev/content/nextjs) — Top resources, templates, courses, and jobs for Next.js.
- Awesome Next.js (https://awesomenextjs.com/) — Curated list of Next.js resources.

### JavaScript & Web Fundamentals

- The Modern JavaScript Tutorial (https://javascript.info/) — Comprehensive, beginner-to-advanced JavaScript tutorial.
- Epic React Articles (https://epicreact.dev/articles) — Articles from Kent C. Dodds' Epic React workshop series.
- Complete Intro to React v7 (https://btholt.github.io/complete-intro-to-react-v7) — Brian Holt's free React course.

### Frontend Architecture & Patterns

- Frontend at Scale (https://frontendatscale.com/) — Level up your frontend architecture skills.
- Refactoring Guru (https://refactoring.guru/) — Design patterns and refactoring techniques.
- Jamstack Generators (https://jamstack.org/generators/) — Compare every static site generator.

---

## Blog: Developers & Engineers to Follow

Programmers who've contributed to and built some of the best tech — follow them to level up.

### React & Frontend Fundamentals

- Josh W Comeau (https://www.joshwcomeau.com/) — CSS, React, and interactive tutorials.
- Dan Abramov (https://overreacted.io/) — React core team. Deep writing about React internals.
- Mark Erikson (https://blog.isquaredsoftware.com/) — Redux maintainer.
- Kent C. Dodds (https://www.epicai.pro/posts) — Testing, React patterns, and AI engineering.
- Robin Wieruch (https://www.robinwieruch.de/) — Practical React and JavaScript tutorials.

### Expo & React Native

- Evan Bacon (https://evanbacon.dev/) — Expo core team.
- Fernando Rojo (https://fernandorojo.co/conf) — Built a startup from zero to $10M with React Native + Next.js.

### Next.js & Vercel

- Lee Robinson (https://leerob.com/vercel) — VP of Product at Vercel.
- Guillermo Rauch (https://rauchg.com/) — CEO of Vercel, creator of Next.js.

### Web Performance & Architecture

- Addy Osmani (https://addyosmani.com/) — Chrome team at Google. Web performance and design patterns.
- Steve Kinney (https://stevekinney.com/) — Frontend architect. React state management and performance.

### Framework & Ecosystem Contributors

- Ben Holmes (https://bholmes.dev/) — Astro core team.
- Anthony Fu (https://antfu.me/) — Vue, Vite, Nuxt core team.

---

## Blog: Engineering Blogs from Top Teams

Blogs that help you think at scale — how top teams build, ship, and solve real problems.

### React Native Ecosystem & Tooling

- Expo (https://expo.dev/blog) — Official Expo blog. SDK releases, EAS updates, universal React patterns.
- Callstack (https://www.callstack.com/blog) — Core React Native contributors. Performance, debugging, New Architecture.
- Software Mansion (https://blog.swmansion.com/) — Maintainers of Reanimated, Gesture Handler, and Screens.
- React Native (https://reactnative.dev/blog) — Official release announcements and architecture updates.

### React — Frontend at Scale

- Airbnb Engineering (https://medium.com/airbnb-engineering) — React at scale, smooth upgrades, and open-source tools.
- Netflix TechBlog (https://netflixtechblog.com/) — High-performance UIs in React, micro frontends.
- Uber Engineering (https://www.uber.com/blog/engineering/) — Base Web design system, Fusion.js.
- Stripe Engineering (https://stripe.com/blog/engineering) — Migrating 3.7M lines from Flow to TypeScript.
- Spotify Engineering (https://engineering.atspotify.com/) — Web player with React + Redux, TypeScript-first architecture.

### React Native — Big Tech & Scale

- Meta Engineering (https://engineering.fb.com/) — Creators of React Native. Performance at scale.
- Microsoft React Native DevBlog (https://devblogs.microsoft.com/react-native/) — React Native on Windows and macOS.
- Shopify Engineering (https://shopify.engineering/topics/mobile) — Five years of React Native at Shopify.
- Coinbase (https://www.coinbase.com/blog/landing) — Migrated 56M users from native to React Native.
- Discord Engineering (https://discord.com/category/engineering) — Native iOS performance with React Native.

### Next.js & Modern Web

- Vercel (https://vercel.com/blog) — Creators of Next.js. React Server Components, Turbopack, caching.
- Next.js (https://nextjs.org/blog) — Official release notes and migration guides.
